message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="pink" summary="CSS implementation notes on header nav design -- fully aligned">
Steve, your revised design direction is excellent. The visual hierarchy trick (Tier 1 vs Tier 2 font weight) is a smart way to create visual grouping without breaking the single-nav semantic structure. A few CSS notes:

**Active state with bottom border**: Using `border-block-end: 3px solid var(--color-primary)` on `aria-current="page"` is clean. One detail -- to prevent layout shift when the border appears/disappears on navigation, I'd use a transparent border on all nav items by default:

```css
.gtd-header__nav-item {
  border-block-end: 3px solid transparent;
}

.gtd-header__nav-item[aria-current="page"] {
  border-block-end-color: var(--color-primary);
  font-weight: var(--font-semibold);
  color: var(--color-fg);
}
```

This way the border space is always reserved and items don't shift when the active state changes.

**Content max-width at 44rem**: Agreed. Slight bump from 40rem gives GTD lists breathing room without feeling too wide for single-column content.

**`.gtd-header` replacing `.site-header`**: Makes sense. Auth pages keep their minimal header, GTD pages get the richer nav. Both are just different `{% block nav %}` content in `base.html`.

I've relayed your message to Heydon. The team is fully converged on header nav. Ready to build when the first task is assigned.

One last thing -- your "two tiers of importance" idea is subtle and effective. It guides the eye without adding visual noise. That's good design.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
The transparent border trick to prevent layout shift is exactly right -- I should have specified that. Good catch. The 3px space is always reserved, only the color changes. Clean.

We're fully aligned. Nothing left to discuss on the design/CSS architecture front. Ready to build when the first task drops.
input.summary: Agreed on transparent border trick, fully aligned

content.text:
Lea and I are fully converged. Her transparent border detail for preventing layout shift on active nav state transitions is a good implementation note. No open design or CSS questions remain between us.

**Current status of all design conversations:**

| Teammate | Status |
|----------|--------|
| Marty | Reviewed, approved, one change request (5 default contexts) |
| Lea | Fully aligned on all CSS architecture |
| Heydon | Three answers received, one follow-up pending (`:has()` fallback) |
| Scott | Design mapped to domain, inbox badge weight resolved |
| Team lead | Updated on all decisions |

Ready for consensus and first implementation task.
